articles.title: Is there still room for conservative treatment of Distal Biceps Tendon ruptures? What patients (and surgeons) should know. A case series with a mean 4-years follow-up.
articles.authors: Coliva F, Marinelli A, Riva M, Valentina V, Tortorella F, Minerba M, et al.
articles.population: Adults with distal biceps tendon rupture treated conservatively
articles.outcomes: Strength deficits (supination 11%, flexion 4-5%), pain during repetitive activities (22.5%), DASH score 10.1, MEPS score 97.5 at mean 47.8 months follow-up
articles.take_home: Conservative treatment for distal biceps tendon rupture works reasonably well in people with sedentary jobs, with only small strength losses. However, people with heavy or repetitive manual work experienced worse outcomes and more pain, so they should consider surgery instead.

articles.title: Propensity Matched Professional Baseball Pitchers With Early Trunk Rotation Demonstrate Increased Shoulder and Elbow Distractive Forces with Equivalent Ball Velocity.
articles.authors: Manzi J, Dillon M, Dowling B, Eskew J, Patel S, Kunze K, et al.
articles.sample_size: 339 pitchers evaluated; 33 early trunk rotation pitchers matched with 132 late trunk rotation pitchers
articles.outcomes: Shoulder and elbow distractive forces, trunk rotation velocity, kinematic variables, ball velocity, and prior upper-extremity injury prevalence
articles.take_home: Pitchers who started rotating their trunk before their front foot landed showed greater stress on their shoulder and elbow joints but threw the ball at the same speed as those who rotated later, suggesting their arms were working harder to achieve the same result. Coaches should be aware that early trunk rotation may place unnecessary strain on the throwing arm.

articles.title: All-Suture Anchors Exhibit Less Perianchor Fluid Formation Independent of Healing Quality Compared With PEEK Anchors in Arthroscopic Rotator Cuff Repair: A Propensity Score-Matched Comparative Study.
articles.authors: Gul O, Ayazoglu MA, Yazici A, Durusoy S, Gurun E, Okutan AE
articles.study_type: Retrospective comparative study with propensity score matching
articles.population: Patients undergoing primary arthroscopic rotator cuff repair with all-suture anchors or PEEK anchors
articles.outcomes: Clinical outcomes (pain, shoulder function, range of motion), tendon healing rates, perianchor fluid collection on MRI at minimum 2 years follow-up
articles.take_home: Both all-suture and PEEK anchors achieved similar healing rates and clinical improvement after rotator cuff repair. All-suture anchors caused less fluid collection around the anchor site, though this fluid collection was more closely linked to healing problems with PEEK anchors.

articles.title: Towards a common definition of frozen shoulder: a scoping review of randomized controlled trials.
articles.authors: Karasuyama M, Ishikawa H, Uchida T, Saka M, Muraki T, Minamikawa T, et al.
articles.study_type: Scoping review of randomised controlled trials
articles.population: Adults with frozen shoulder or adhesive capsulitis
articles.sample_size: 310 randomised controlled trials analysed from 4,426 records identified
articles.outcomes: How frozen shoulder is defined across trials, including terminology used, age criteria, pain characteristics, range of motion limitations, symptom duration, and imaging requirements
articles.take_home: Frozen shoulder is defined very differently across research trials, making it hard to compare results between studies. Key areas needing standardisation include what symptoms and movement losses doctors should measure, how long symptoms should have lasted, and what age groups to include.

articles.title: Temporal trends and estimated lifetime attributable radiation risk of preoperative planning computed tomography for primary shoulder arthroplasty.
articles.authors: Kalva SR, Fucich D, Perry AJ, Lezak B, Torkieh J, Joshi T, et al.
articles.outcomes: Annual percentage of patients receiving preoperative planning CT scans; estimated lifetime attributable risk of cancer from radiation exposure stratified by age and sex
articles.take_home: The use of CT scans before shoulder replacement surgery has increased from 53% to 66% of patients between 2016 and 2024. These scans carry a small but measurable risk of causing cancer in the future, with younger women having the highest estimated risk.

articles.title: Shoulder Muscle Adiposity Can Predict the Efficacy of Corticosteroid Injections in Treating Adhesive Capsulitis After Rotator Cuff Repair Surgery.
articles.authors: Chen NH, Lin CL, Cheng YH, Chiu YS, Hsu TH, Huang SW
articles.population: Patients who developed adhesive capsulitis (shoulder stiffness) after rotator cuff repair surgery and received corticosteroid injection
articles.outcomes: Pain scores and range of motion at 8 weeks after corticosteroid injection; preoperative shoulder muscle fatty infiltration measured on MRI
articles.take_home: Patients with higher amounts of fatty tissue in their shoulder muscles before surgery were more likely to have corticosteroid injections fail to relieve post-surgery stiffness. This measurement could help doctors identify patients who might need different treatment earlier.

articles.title: Is a 32 mm glenosphere optimal in reverse shoulder arthroplasty for patients with small glenoid anatomy? : a range of motion simulation study in petite Asian females.
articles.authors: Kiyomatsu H, Fujibuchi T, Kutsuna T, Kinoshita T, Tsuda T, Hayashi M, et al.
articles.study_type: Computer simulation study using 3D CT planning
articles.population: Asian female patients with small glenoid anatomy undergoing reverse shoulder arthroplasty
articles.sample_size: 11 patients with postoperative outcomes; 3D simulations performed on patient CT scans
articles.outcomes: Impingement-free range of motion in extension-flexion, abduction-adduction, and external-internal rotation with different glenosphere sizes (32 mm, 36 mm, 39 mm) and inferior overhang positions
articles.take_home: In patients with small glenoid bones, using a smaller 32 mm glenosphere with adequate positioning gave the best simulated range of shoulder motion compared to larger sizes. The findings suggest this approach may be better for small glenoid anatomy, but real patient results need to be confirmed.

articles.title: Does robust greater tuberosity repair affect the outcomes of reverse shoulder replacement in proximal humerus fractures? - A Bayesian network meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Ponugoti N, Selim A, Elhassan A, Jeavons R
articles.population: Older adults with proximal humerus fractures treated with reverse shoulder arthroplasty
articles.outcomes: Constant score (shoulder function measure) and shoulder motion (abduction, external rotation, forward flexion)
articles.take_home: Repairing the greater tuberosity (a bone fragment) during reverse shoulder replacement for upper arm fractures improves arm function and movement, but only if the repair heals properly. Not repairing it or having an unsuccessful repair produced similar results.

articles.title: The meta-analysis and systematic review of arthroscopic lower trapezius tendon transfer for massive irreparable rotator cuff tears.
articles.authors: Baek CH, Kim JG, Kim BT, Lim C
articles.population: Patients with massive irreparable posterosuperior rotator cuff tears treated with arthroscopically assisted lower trapezius tendon transfer
articles.outcomes: External rotation improved by 27.9 degrees, forward flexion by 37.0 degrees, pain scores reduced by 4.2 points, shoulder function scores improved by 32.5 points on ASES and 22.7 points on Constant score. Graft healing rate 89.4%, complication rate 3.70%, reoperation rate 3.46%, conversion to reverse shoulder replacement 2.08%
articles.take_home: Transferring the lower trapezius tendon can successfully improve shoulder movement, reduce pain, and restore function in patients with massive irreparable rotator cuff tears, with good healing rates and low complication rates. This procedure appears to work well across different patient ages and graft types.
